DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL PADWAY, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MICHAEL PADWAY & ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. COUNSEL STIPULATE TO HAVING THE MATTER DECIDED BY JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PRODUCE THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR ATTORNEYS IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00. ORDER SIGNED IN COURT BUT NOT PROVIDED TO THE CLERK FOR FILING. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE, NOT REPORTED

ALTHOUGH TITLED A "MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF PRIVILEGE OBJECTIONS," DEFENDANTS MOVE FOR RECONSIDERATION OF AN ORDER ISSUED BY PRO TEM JUDGE RENNE ON JANUARY 6, 2014. PRO TEM JUDGE PAUL RENNE, A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE BAR WHO MEETS ALL THE REQUIREMENTS SET FORTH IN CRC 2.812 TO SERVE AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE, WAS ASSIGNED TO HEAR THE UNDERLYING MOTION. ON JANUARY 6, 2014, THE PARTIES STIPULATED TO PRO TEM JUDGE RENNE HEARING THE UNDERLYING MOTION THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF RECONSIDERATION. THE PARTIES ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE AUTHORITY OF PRO TEM JUDGE RENNE FOR THE INSTANT MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EHG)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Off Calendar

Defendant Napoli Bern Ripka & Associates, Llp'S Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And Dba Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kelly McMeekin, Esq. of Napoli Bern Ripka Shkolnik & Associates LLP, Tel. 310.331.8224, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its uncontested tentative ruling on DEFENDANT NAPOLI BERN RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLP'S Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And DBA Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions. DEFENDANT' MOTION TO BE RELIEVED FROM THIS COURT'S PRIOR ORDER RE: WAIVER OF PRIVILEGE OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT HAS MADE A SHOWING OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CALIF. CODE OF CIVIL PROC. SECTION 473(b). NOTHING IN THIS ORDER IS INTENDED TO ADDRESS THE MERITS OF DEFENDANT'S PRIVILEGE CLAIMS. THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00 IS AFFIRMED AND PAYMENT SHOULD BE MADE TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN TEN (DAYS) FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. (COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT TO THE COURT OF SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON FEBRUARY 25, 2014.) Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And Dba Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT NAPOLI BERN RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLP's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And Dba Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions - To be heard before Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne on February 25, 2014 at 9:00 am in Department 302 per agreement of the parties. Although titled a "Motion for Relief from Waiver of Privilege Objections," Defendants move for reconsideration of an order issued by Pro Tem Judge Renne on January 6, 2014. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, was assigned to hear the underlying motion. On January 6, 2014, the parties stipulated to Pro Tem Judge Renne hearing the underlying motion that is the subject of reconsideration. The parties are deemed to have stipulated to the authority of Pro Tem Judge Renne for the instant motion for reconsideration. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL PADWAY, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MICHAEL PADWAY & ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS

Appearances: E. Page Allinson of Napoli Bern Ripka Shkolnik & Associates LLP (310.331.8224) for defendant and responding party; Michael Padway of Michael Padway & Associates (415.777.6541) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona.

Counsel stipulate to having the matter decided by Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne. Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: No opposition having been filed, plaintiff's motion to compel production of documents is granted. Defendants are ordered to produce the requested documents without objection within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order. The court will impose monetary sanctions against defendants and their attorneys in the amount of $250.00. Order signed in court but not provided to the clerk for filing. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Not reported (302/JPT)
